what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment metadata record that associates an attachment (att-c60ae1fe) in the dragnet-development channel with a board item target (board://item-de1fb9fb) and records when it was added.
observations: The file consists only of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
It declares an attachment_type of board_items.
The target is expressed as a board:// URI pointing to item-de1fb9fb.
No code symbols, descriptions, or payloads are present.
standouts: The target uses a board:// URI scheme to reference an external board item.
The file is purely structural metadata with zero explanatory content.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with standard metadata keys: id, channel, attachment_type, target, added.
References another artifact via a URI-style identifier rather than a file path.
Leaves the body empty, treating the file as a typed index entry.
concerns: The attachment carries no payload, summary, or context about what it represents.
The referenced board item is not described or included, so the file is not self-explanatory.
notes: A bare channel-attachment index entry that links dragnet-development attachment att-c60ae1fe to board item item-de1fb9fb.
questions: What content or discussion does this attachment capture?
What is the nature of board://item-de1fb9fb?
Why does the attachment have no body or descriptive payload?
intention_signals: It appears intended to register a persistent association between a channel attachment and a board item so the relationship can be looked up later.
novelty: The use of a board:// URI as the target of a channel attachment is the only distinctive feature; otherwise it is a conventional metadata stub.
contribution: It preserves the fact that dragnet-development attachment att-c60ae1fe was added on 2026-06-24T15:14:05.873128+00:00 and targeted board item board://item-de1fb9fb.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-only channel attachment metadata file linking dragnet-development attachment att-c60ae1fe to board item board://item-de1fb9fb with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to record an association between a channel attachment and a specific board item for retrieval or audit.
